title: AssemblyConstraints Object
description: The collection of assembly constraints in this component. This provides access to all existing assembly constraints and supports the ability to create new assembly constraints.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: createInput
methods.description: Creates an assembly constraint input to define an assembly constraint. Use functionality on the returned AssemblyConstraintInput to define the input needed to create an assembly constraint.

methods.name: item
methods.description: Function that returns the specified assembly constraint using an index into the collection.

methods.name: itemByName
methods.description: Function that returns the specified assembly constraint object using a name.

properties.name: count
properties.description: Returns the number of assembly constraint objects in the collection.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

add.title: AssemblyConstraints.add Method
add.return_description: Returns the newly created AssemblyConstraint or null in the case of failure.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: The AssemblyConstraintInput object that defines the geometry and various inputs that define an assembly constraint. An AssemblyConstraintInput object is created using the AssemblyConstraints.createInput method.

classType.title: AssemblyConstraints.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

createInput.title: AssemblyConstraints.createInput Method
createInput.description: Creates an assembly constraint input to define an assembly constraint. Use functionality on the returned AssemblyConstraintInput to define the input needed to create an assembly constraint.

item.title: AssemblyConstraints.item Method
item.description: Function that returns the specified assembly constraint using an index into the collection.
item.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the item within the collection to return. The first item in the collection has an index of 0.

itemByName.title: AssemblyConstraints.itemByName Method
itemByName.description: Function that returns the specified assembly constraint object using a name.
itemByName.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid name was specified.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the item within the collection to return.

count.title: AssemblyConstraints.count Property
count.description: Returns the number of assembly constraint objects in the collection.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = assemblyConstraints_var.count

isValid.title: AssemblyConstraints.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = assemblyConstraints_var.isValid

objectType.title: AssemblyConstraints.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = assemblyConstraints_var.objectType
